title: Empirical Psychology
definition: The methodical study of the mind using direct observation and experimentation, as employed by the Buddha to discover the path leading to enlightenment.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Exploring the mind scientifically, like a scientist investigating the world around us.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Investigating mental processes through direct observation and experimentation, focusing on understanding the mechanisms of the mind.
difficulty_levels.expert: The application of empirical methods to the study of the mind, emphasizing the discovery of psychological principles through systematic observation and experimentation, as demonstrated by the Buddha's journey to enlightenment.

extra_fields.insight: The realization that the path to enlightenment can be discovered and followed using a scientific approach.
